# fargv — LLM reference

> Zero-friction Python CLI parser. Define parameters once as a plain dict,
> dataclass, or function signature; fargv infers types, generates --help,
> reads config files, applies env-var overrides, and optionally opens a GUI.
> All styles return `(namespace, help_str)`.

Machine-readable reference: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/anguelos/fargv/main/llms.txt
Docs: https://fargv.readthedocs.io
PyPI: https://pypi.org/project/fargv/
Source: https://github.com/anguelos/fargv

```
pip install fargv
```

---

## Choosing a definition style

fargv.parse() accepts four definition forms. Pick one:

| Style | Best for | Returns |
|---|---|---|
| Plain dict | prototypes, scripts, notebooks | SimpleNamespace |
| Dict with Fargv* types | descriptions, rich types, mandatory params | SimpleNamespace |
| Dataclass | typed result, IDE autocompletion, reusable config | dataclass instance |
| Function signature | exposing callables, parse_and_launch | SimpleNamespace |

All styles:
- Use `--name=value` (double-dash) CLI syntax with auto-inferred `-s` short aliases
- Get `--help`, `--verbosity`, `--bash_autocomplete`, `--config` for free
- Support env-var overrides (`APPNAME_PARAMNAME`) and JSON/YAML/TOML/INI config files
- Return `(namespace, help_str)` — override shape with `return_type=`

---

## Style 1 — Plain dict

Types inferred from Python type of the default value.

```python
import fargv

p, _ = fargv.parse({
    "name":    "world",                        # str
    "count":   1,                              # int
    "lr":      0.001,                          # float
    "verbose": False,                          # bool flag
    "mode":    ("fast", "slow", "medium"),     # choice; first = default
    "base":    "/tmp",
    "out":     "{base}/results",              # {key} resolved from sibling str params
    "files":   [],                             # variadic list (unmatched tokens)
})
# python script.py --name=Alice --count=3 --verbose --mode=slow a.txt b.txt
```

**When**: minimum boilerplate, self-documenting defaults, research/notebook scripts.
**Limit**: no per-param descriptions, no rich types, no mandatory params, `p.lr` is `Any`.

> **Two-element tuple pitfall** — `("fast", "slow")` -> `(default="fast", description="slow")`,
> a plain string param. Use >=3 elements for a binary choice, or use `FargvChoice`.

---

## Style 2 — Dict with Fargv* types

Mix plain literals and explicit `Fargv*` objects freely in one dict.

```python
import sys, fargv

p, _ = fargv.parse({
    "weights":  fargv.FargvStr(fargv.REQUIRED,
                    description="Pretrained weights path (mandatory)"),
    "epochs":   fargv.FargvInt(100, description="Training epochs"),
    "lr":       fargv.FargvFloat(0.01),
    "arch":     fargv.FargvChoice(["resnet50", "vit_b16", "efficientnet"],
                    description="Model architecture"),
    "img_size": fargv.FargvTuple((int, int), default=(640, 640)),
    "log":      fargv.FargvStream(sys.stderr),
    "verbose":  fargv.FargvInt(0, short_name="v", is_count_switch=True),
    "ckpts":    fargv.FargvVariadic(default=[], description="Extra checkpoints"),
    "dry_run":  False,                          # plain literal still works
})
# python train.py --weights=model.pt --lr=1e-4 -vv a.pt b.pt
```

**When**: --help quality matters, some params must exist on disk, streams, fixed-length
tuples, count-switches, or mandatory params.
**Limit**: still returns SimpleNamespace (no IDE autocompletion on result).

---

## Style 3 — Dataclass

Pass the **class** (not an instance). Return value is an instance of your class.

```python
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import sys, fargv

@dataclass
class Config:
    data_root:  str   = "/datasets/imagenet"
    arch:       str   = "resnet50"
    epochs:     int   = 90
    lr:         float = 0.1
    amp:        bool  = False
    # Rich types: assign Fargv* instance as default
    verbose:    int   = fargv.FargvInt(0, short_name="v", is_count_switch=True)
    log:        object = fargv.FargvStream(sys.stderr)
    files:      list  = fargv.FargvVariadic(default=[])

cfg, _ = fargv.parse(Config)
# cfg is Config -- IDE autocompletes cfg.lr, cfg.arch
# isinstance(cfg, Config) == True
# python train.py --arch=vit_b16 --epochs=30 --amp
```

**Mandatory fields** -- omit the default entirely:

```python
@dataclass
class InferConfig:
    checkpoint: str           # no default -> required on CLI
    threshold:  float = 0.5
```

**Field docstrings** -- bare string literal immediately after field definition:

```python
@dataclass
class Cfg:
    lr: float = 0.1
    "Initial learning rate."            # shown in --help; next-line style
```

**When**: typed result, IDE autocompletion, mypy/pyright, config reused across modules.
**Limit**: type annotations required for accurate coercion.

---

## Style 4 — Function signature

Pass any callable; fargv inspects `typing.get_type_hints` and `inspect.signature`.

```python
import fargv

def train(
    corpus:    str,
    tokeniser: str  = "wordpiece",
    vocab:     int  = 30_000,
    lower:     bool = True,
) -> None:
    pass

p, _ = fargv.parse(train)
train(**vars(p))
# python tok.py --corpus=/data/wiki.txt --vocab=50000

# One-liner: parse + call
fargv.parse_and_launch(train)

# parse_here() inside the function itself
def run(lr: float = 0.01, epochs: int = 10):
    p, _ = fargv.parse_here()   # resolves own signature
```

**Mandatory params** (no default):

```python
def run(host: str, port: int = 8080): ...
p, _ = fargv.parse(run, non_defaults_are_mandatory=True)
```

**Tuple/Optional[Tuple] annotations** auto-map to FargvTuple:

```python
from typing import Tuple, Optional
def crop(size: Tuple[int,int] = (224,224),
         region: Optional[Tuple[int,int,int,int]] = None): ...
# --size=(640,480)   --region=(0,0,224,224)   --region=() -> None
```

**Invoke any importable callable without a script:**

```bash
python -m fargv numpy.linspace --start=0 --stop=6.283 --num=8
python -m fargv mypackage.train --lr=0.001 --epochs=50
```

**When**: library function you want to expose as CLI, python -m fargv ad-hoc use.
**Limit**: rich Fargv* types not usable as defaults; unannotated None defaults skipped.

---

## Legacy API -- backwards compatibility only

`fargv.fargv()` uses single-dash `-name=value`. Frozen; no new features.
For new code: use `fargv.parse`.

```python
import fargv
p, help_str = fargv.fargv({
    "name":    "world",
    "count":   1,
    "verbose": False,
    "mode":    ("fast", "slow", "medium"),
    "files":   set(),          # set() = variadic in legacy
})
# python script.py -name=Alice -count=3 -verbose -mode=slow a.txt
```

Signature: `fargv(default_switches, argv=None, use_enviromental_variables=True,`
`  return_type="SimpleNamespace", spaces_are_equals=True, description=None)`

Built-in legacy flags: `-help`/`-h`, `-bash_autocomplete`, `-v` (verbosity int).
Env-var names: bare parameter name (`count=42 python script.py`).

Full reference: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api_legacy.html

---

## Type-inference table (plain dict and function styles)

`bool` matched before `int` (bool is int subclass).

| Default / annotation | Fargv* class | CLI |
|---|---|---|
| `False`/`True`/`bool` | FargvBool | `--verbose` or `--verbose=false` |
| `0`, `42` / `int` | FargvInt | `--count=3` |
| `0.0` / `float` | FargvFloat | `--lr=1e-4` |
| `"hi"` / `str` | FargvStr | `--name=Alice` |
| `("a","b","c")` >=3 items | FargvChoice | `--mode=b` |
| `(default, "desc")` 2-item | FargvX with description | — |
| `[]` / `list` | FargvVariadic | trailing tokens |
| `set()` | FargvVariadic | same (legacy compat) |
| `Tuple[T,...]` annotation | FargvTuple | `--size=(640,480)` |
| `Optional[Tuple[T,...]]` | FargvTuple(optional=True) | `--size=()` -> None |

---

## All Fargv* parameter classes

All exported from `fargv` top-level.

| Class | Key kwargs | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `FargvBool(default)` | short_name, description | bare flag toggles; `--v false` clears |
| `FargvInt(default)` | short_name, is_count_switch, description | `-vvv`=3 when count_switch |
| `FargvFloat(default)` | short_name, description | |
| `FargvStr(default)` | short_name, description | `{key}` interpolation from siblings |
| `FargvChoice(choices)` | default (first if omitted), description | choices = list |
| `FargvVariadic(default=[])` | description | collects unmatched CLI tokens |
| `FargvInputStream(default=stdin)` | description | CLI: path or "stdin" |
| `FargvOutputStream(default=stdout)` | description | CLI: path, "stdout", or "stderr" |
| `FargvStream(default)` | description | pass sys.stdin/out/err |
| `FargvPath(default)` | description | returns pathlib.Path |
| `FargvExistingFile(default)` | description | path must exist |
| `FargvNonExistingFile(default)` | description | path must not exist |
| `FargvFile(default)` | description | parent dir must exist |
| `FargvTuple(elem_types, default)` | optional, description | via ast.literal_eval |
| `FargvSubcommand(definitions)` | | nested dict or auto-inferred |
| `REQUIRED` | — | sentinel; makes param mandatory |

---

## fargv.parse() -- full signature

```python
def parse(
    definition,                         # dict | Callable | dataclass | ArgumentParser
    given_parameters=None,              # None->sys.argv | List[str]->argv | Dict->direct
    argv_parse_mode="unix",             # "unix" (--) or "legacy" (-)
    allow_implied_variadics=True,
    tolerate_unassigned_arguments=False,
    ui=None,                            # None|"cli"|"tk"|"qt"|"jupyter"
    auto_define_help=True,
    auto_define_bash_autocomplete=True,
    auto_define_verbosity=True,
    auto_define_config=True,
    auto_define_user_interface=True,
    colored_help=None,                  # None=auto-detect TTY
    employ_docstring_in_help=True,
    return_type="SimpleNamespace",      # "SimpleNamespace"|"dict"|"namedtuple"|"namespace"
    subcommand_return_type="flat",      # "flat"|"nested"|"tuple"
    non_defaults_are_mandatory=False,
    fn_def_tolerate_wildcards=False,
    override_order=["default","config","envvar","ui"],
) -> (namespace, help_str)
```

### given_parameters

| Value | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `None` | reads sys.argv |
| `List[str]` | parsed as argv; first element = program name |
| `Dict[str,Any]` | applied directly; bypasses all CLI/GUI parsing |

### subcommand_return_type

| Value | `p.cmd` | Sub-params |
|---|---|---|
| `"flat"` | `"train"` (string) | merged into top namespace |
| `"nested"` | `SimpleNamespace(name="train", lr=0.5)` | inside p.cmd |
| `"tuple"` | — | returns `((name, sub_ns, parent_ns), help_str)` |

Use `"nested"` or `"tuple"` with dataclass definitions.

### Override order

`coded default -> config file -> env vars -> CLI/GUI`

Env-var: `APPNAME_PARAMNAME` (uppercased; APPNAME from argv[0], dots/hyphens → underscores).

**Flat key convention for config and env vars.**
Top-level param `lr` → config key `lr` / env var `APPNAME_LR`.
Branch param `lr` inside subcommand branch `train` → config key `train.lr` / env var `APPNAME_TRAIN_LR`.
The subcommand field name itself is not a valid config key or env var target.
Config files: unknown keys trigger a stderr warning and the whole dict is ignored (default policy).

### return_type

| Value | Type of `p` |
|---|---|
| `"SimpleNamespace"` (default) | types.SimpleNamespace |
| `"dict"` | dict |
| `"namedtuple"` | named tuple |
| `"namespace"` | FargvNamespace (observable, linkable to GUI backends) |
| *(dataclass input)* | dataclass instance (ignores return_type) |

### Auto-injected flags (stripped from result)

| Flag | Alias | Effect |
|---|---|---|
| `--help` | `-h` | coloured help, exit |
| `--verbosity` | `-v` | count-switch; `-vvv`=3 |
| `--bash_autocomplete` | — | bash completion, exit |
| `--config` | `-C` | load JSON/YAML/TOML/INI; `//json`/`//ini`/`//toml`/`//yaml` dumps config and exits |
| `--user_interface` | — | `cli`/`tk`/`qt`; suppressed in Jupyter |

---

## Common patterns

```python
# Minimal script
p, _ = fargv.parse({"n": 1, "verbose": False})

# Function-based, parse + call
fargv.parse_and_launch(train)

# Mandatory params via function
def run(host: str, port: int = 8080): ...
p, _ = fargv.parse(run, non_defaults_are_mandatory=True)

# Subcommands
p, _ = fargv.parse({"cmd": {"train": {"lr": 0.01}, "eval": {"dataset": "val"}}})
# python prog.py train --lr=0.5  ->  p.cmd=="train", p.lr==0.5

# Pre-parsed dict (tests / notebooks)
p, _ = fargv.parse({"lr": 0.001}, given_parameters={"lr": 5e-4})

# Mix plain literals + explicit types
p, _ = fargv.parse({
    "model": fargv.FargvExistingFile(fargv.REQUIRED),
    "out":   fargv.FargvFile("/tmp/result.pt"),
    "lr":    0.001,
})

# Typed dataclass result
from dataclasses import dataclass
@dataclass
class Cfg:
    lr: float = 1e-3
    epochs: int = 10
cfg, _ = fargv.parse(Cfg)
assert isinstance(cfg, Cfg)

# Dump config to stdout and exit (//format syntax)
# $ python prog.py --config //json   →  prints JSON to stdout, suggests save path to stderr
# $ python prog.py --config //ini    →  INI format with ; comments
# $ python prog.py -C //yaml         →  YAML format (-C is short alias for --config)

# Flat key convention: subcommand branch 'train', param 'lr'
# Config file key:  train.lr        (dot separator)
# Env var:          PROG_TRAIN_LR   (underscore separator, with app prefix)

# Unknown keys in config: warns to stderr, ignores entire dict (default)
# Change with: fargv.parse(..., unknown_keys="ignore_key_and_warn")
```

---

## FargvParameter API (for library authors)

```python
param.value          # current value after parsing
param.default        # original default
param.name           # long name
param.short_name     # single-char alias or None
param.description    # help text or None
param._mandatory     # True when created with REQUIRED
param.has_value      # False if mandatory and not yet set
param.evaluate(val)              # set from Python object
param.ingest_value_strings(*s)   # set from CLI tokens; returns leftover list
param.docstring(colored=None)    # formatted --help line
```

---

## ArgumentParser (low-level)

```python
from fargv.parser import ArgumentParser
from fargv.parameters import FargvInt, FargvStr

parser = ArgumentParser(long_prefix="--", short_prefix="-")
parser._add_parameter(FargvInt(0, name="count", short_name="n"))
parser._add_parameter(FargvStr("", name="label"))
parser.infer_short_names()

result_dict = parser.parse(["prog", "--count=3"])   # returns dict
help_str = parser.generate_help_message(colored=None, verbosity=1)

# Wrap with fargv.parse() to get auto-params, config, env-vars
p, _ = fargv.parse(parser)
```

---

## Sphinx docs (deeper reference)

- Quickstart: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/quickstart.html
- Definition styles: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/defining_parsers.html
- Parameter types: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/parameter_types.html
- Subcommands: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/subcommands.html
- Config & env vars: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/config_envvars.html
- Return types: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/return_types.html
- GUI backends: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/gui_backends.html
- Legacy API: https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/api_legacy.html
- CLI tool (python -m fargv): https://fargv.readthedocs.io/en/latest/cli.html
